children.heading: Areas, locations, and institutions lacking broadband access
children.label: 1 Areas, locations, and institutions lacking broadband access
i
children.text: has no access to broadband service; or

children.label: aa
children.text: 25 megabits per second for downloads; and

children.label: bb
children.text: 3 megabits per second for uploads; and

children.label: II
children.text: a latency sufficient to support real-time, interactive applications.

children.heading: Unserved service project
children.label: B Unserved service project
children.text: The term “unserved service project” means a project in which not less than 80 percent of broadband-serviceable locations served by the project are unserved locations.

children.label: i
children.text: that is not an unserved location; and

children.label: aa
children.text: 100 megabits per second for downloads; and

children.label: bb
children.text: 20 megabits per second for uploads; and

children.label: II
children.text: a latency sufficient to support real-time, interactive applications.

children.heading: Underserved service project
children.label: D Underserved service project
children.text: The term “underserved service project” means a project in which not less than 80 percent of broadband-serviceable locations served by the project are unserved locations or underserved locations.

children.heading: Eligible community anchor institution
children.label: E Eligible community anchor institution
children.text: The term “eligible community anchor institution” means a community anchor institution that lacks access to gigabit-level broadband service.

children.heading: Assistant Secretary
children.label: A Assistant Secretary
children.text: The term “Assistant Secretary” means the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information.

children.heading: Broadband; broadband service
children.label: B Broadband; broadband service
children.text: The term “broadband” or “broadband service” has the meaning given the term “broadband internet access service” in section 8.1(b) of title 47, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulation.

children.heading: Broadband DATA maps
children.label: C Broadband DATA maps
children.text: The term “broadband DATA maps” means the maps created under section 802(c)(1) of the Communications Act of 1934 ().47 U.S.C. 642(c)(1)

children.heading: Commission
children.label: D Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Federal Communications Commission.

children.heading: Community anchor institution
children.label: E Community anchor institution
children.text: The term “community anchor institution” means an entity such as a school, library, health clinic, health center, hospital or other medical provider, public safety entity, institution of higher education, public housing organization, or community support organization that facilitates greater use of broadband service by vulnerable populations, including low-income individuals, unemployed individuals, and aged individuals.

children.heading: Eligible entity
children.label: F Eligible entity
children.text: The term “eligible entity” means a State.

children.label: I
children.text: the remote location of the area;

children.label: II
children.text: the lack of population density of the area;

children.label: III
children.text: the unique topography of the area;

children.label: IV
children.text: a high rate of poverty in the area; or

children.label: V
children.text: any other factor identified by the Assistant Secretary, in consultation with the Commission, that contributes to the higher cost of deploying broadband service in the area.

children.heading: Unserved area
children.label: ii Unserved area
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the term “unserved area” means an area in which not less than 80 percent of broadband-serviceable locations are unserved locations.

children.heading: Location; broadband-serviceable location
children.label: H Location; broadband-serviceable location
children.text: The terms “location” and “broadband-serviceable location” have the meanings given those terms by the Commission under rules and guidance that are in effect, as of .November 15, 2021

children.label: i
children.text: provide broadband service that meets speed, latency, reliability, consistency in quality of service, and related criteria as the Assistant Secretary shall determine; and

children.label: I
children.text: meet the evolving connectivity needs of households and businesses; and

children.label: II
children.text: support the deployment of 5G, successor wireless technologies, and other advanced services.

children.heading: Program
children.label: J Program
children.text: The term “Program” means the Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment Program established under subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Project
children.label: K Project
children.text: The term “project” means an undertaking by a subgrantee under this section to construct and deploy infrastructure for the provision of broadband service.

children.heading: Reliable broadband service
children.label: L Reliable broadband service
children.text: The term “reliable broadband service” means broadband service that meets performance criteria for service availability, adaptability to changing end-user requirements, length of serviceable life, or other criteria, other than upload and download speeds, as determined by the Assistant Secretary in coordination with the Commission.

children.heading: State
children.label: M State
children.text: The term “State” has the meaning given the term in , except that that definition shall be applied by striking “, and any other territory or possession of the United States”.section 942 of this title

children.heading: Subgrantee
children.label: N Subgrantee
children.text: The term “subgrantee” means an entity that receives grant funds from an eligible entity to carry out activities under subsection (f).

children.heading: Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment Program
children.label: b Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment Program

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Assistant Secretary shall establish a grant program, to be known as the “Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment Program”, under which the Assistant Secretary makes grants to eligible entities, in accordance with this section, to bridge the digital divide.November 15, 2021

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 2 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Assistant Secretary to carry out the Program $42,450,000,000.

children.heading: Obligation timeline
children.label: 3 Obligation timeline
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall obligate all amounts appropriated pursuant to paragraph (2) in an expedient manner after the Assistant Secretary issues the notice of funding opportunity under subsection (e)(1).

children.label: i
children.text: the development of grant applications under the Program;

children.label: ii
children.text: the development of plans and procedures for distribution of funds under the Program; and

children.label: iii
children.text: other technical support as determined by the Assistant Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: expansion in rural areas; and

children.label: II
children.text: eligible entities that consistently rank below most other eligible entities with respect to broadband access and deployment; and

children.label: ii
children.text: regarding cybersecurity resources and programs available through Federal agencies, including the Election Assistance Commission, the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, the Federal Trade Commission, and the National Institute of Standards and Technology.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On or after the date on which the broadband DATA maps are made public, the Assistant Secretary shall allocate to eligible entities, in accordance with subparagraph (B) of this paragraph, 10 percent of the amount appropriated pursuant to subsection (b)(2).

children.label: i
children.text: dividing the number of unserved locations in high-cost areas in the eligible entity by the total number of unserved locations in high-cost areas in the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: multiplying the quotient obtained under clause (i) by the amount made available under subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: except as provided in subparagraph (B) of this paragraph, $100,000,000 shall be allocated to each State; and

children.label: B
children.text: $100,000,000 shall be allocated to, and divided equally among, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On or after the date on which the broadband DATA maps are made public, of the amount appropriated pursuant to subsection (b)(2), the Assistant Secretary shall allocate to eligible entities, in accordance with subparagraph (B) of this paragraph, the amount remaining after compliance with paragraphs (1) and (2) of this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: dividing the number of unserved locations in the eligible entity by the total number of unserved locations in the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: multiplying the quotient obtained under clause (i) by the amount made available under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Availability conditioned on approval of applications
children.label: 4 Availability conditioned on approval of applications
children.text: The availability of amounts allocated under paragraph (1), (2), or (3) to an eligible entity shall be subject to approval by the Assistant Secretary of the letter of intent, initial proposal, or final proposal of the eligible entity, as applicable, under subsection (e).

children.heading: Definition
children.label: A Definition
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “covered application” means a letter of intent, initial proposal, or final proposal under this section.

children.heading: Application failures
children.label: i Application failures
children.text: The Assistant Secretary, in carrying out the Program, shall provide that if an eligible entity fails to submit a covered application by the applicable deadline, or a covered application submitted by an eligible entity is not approved by the applicable deadline, a political subdivision or consortium of political subdivisions of the eligible entity may submit the applicable type of covered application in place of the eligible entity.

children.heading: Treatment of political subdivision or consortium as eligible entity
children.label: ii Treatment of political subdivision or consortium as eligible entity
I
children.text: except as provided in subclause (II) of this clause, any reference in this section to an eligible entity shall be deemed to refer to the political subdivision or consortium; and

children.label: II
children.text: any reference in this section to an eligible entity in a geographic sense shall be deemed to refer to the eligible entity in whose place the political subdivision or consortium submitted the covered application.

children.heading: Reallocation to other eligible entities
children.label: C Reallocation to other eligible entities
I
children.text: shall reallocate the amounts that would have been available to the eligible entity pursuant to that type of covered application to other eligible entities that submitted that type of covered application by the applicable deadline; and

children.label: II
children.text: shall reallocate the amounts described in subclause (I) of this clause in accordance with the formula under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Failure to use full allocation
children.label: ii Failure to use full allocation
I
children.text: shall reallocate the unused amounts to other eligible entities with approved final proposals; and

children.label: II
children.text: shall reallocate the amounts described in subclause (I) in accordance with the formula under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Assistant Secretary
children.label: 1 Assistant Secretary
children.text: The Assistant Secretary may use not more than 2 percent of amounts appropriated pursuant to subsection (b) for administrative purposes.

children.heading: Pre-deployment planning
children.label: A Pre-deployment planning
children.text: An eligible entity may use not more than 5 percent of the amount allocated to the eligible entity under subsection (c)(2) for the planning and pre-deployment activities under subsection (e)(1)(C).

children.heading: Administration
children.label: B Administration
children.text: An eligible entity may use not more than 2 percent of the grant amounts made available to the eligible entity under subsection (e) for expenses relating (directly or indirectly) to administration of the grant.

children.heading: Notice of funding opportunity; process
children.label: A Notice of funding opportunity; process
aa
children.text: the establishment of the Program; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the amount of the minimum initial allocation to each eligible entity under subsection (c)(2);

children.label: aa
children.text: participate in the Program; and

children.label: bb
children.text: receive funding for planning and pre-deployment activities under subparagraph (C);

children.label: aa
children.text: applications for grants under the Program, which shall consist of letters of intent, initial proposals, and final proposals; and

children.label: bb
children.text: allowed uses of grant amounts awarded under this section, as provided in subsection (f); and

children.label: IV
children.text: includes any other information determined relevant by the Assistant Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: establish a process, in accordance with subparagraph (C), through which to provide funding to eligible entities for planning and pre-deployment activities;

children.label: iii
children.text: develop and make public a standard online application form that an eligible entity may use to submit an initial proposal and final proposal for the grant amounts allocated to the eligible entity under subsection (c);

children.label: I
children.text: initial proposal that complies with paragraph (3)(A); and

children.label: II
children.text: final proposal that complies with paragraph (4)(A); and

children.label: v
children.text: in consultation with the Commission, establish standards for how an eligible entity shall assess the capabilities and capacities of a prospective subgrantee under subsection (g)(2)(A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: An eligible entity that wishes to participate in the Program shall file a letter of intent to participate in the Program consistent with this subparagraph.

children.label: AA
children.text: activities that the program or office currently conducts;

children.label: BB
children.text: the number of rounds of broadband deployment grants that the eligible entity has awarded, if applicable;

children.label: CC
children.text: whether the eligible entity has an eligible entity-wide plan and goal for availability of broadband, and any relevant deadlines, as applicable; and

children.label: DD
children.text: the amount of funding that the eligible entity has available for broadband deployment or other broadband-related activities, including data collection and local planning, and the sources of that funding, including whether the funds are from the eligible entity or from the Federal Government under the American Rescue Plan Act of 2021 ();Public Law 117–2

children.label: bb
children.text: the number of full-time employees and part-time employees of the eligible entity who will assist in administering amounts received under the Program and the duties assigned to those employees;

children.label: cc
children.text: relevant contracted support; and

children.label: dd
children.text: the goals of the eligible entity for the use of amounts received under the Program, the process that the eligible entity will use to distribute those amounts to subgrantees, the timeline for awarding subgrants, and oversight and reporting requirements that the eligible entity will impose on subgrantees;

children.label: II
children.text: the identification of known barriers or challenges to developing and administering a program to administer grants received under the Program, if applicable;

children.label: aa
children.text: enhancing the capacity of the broadband program or office of the eligible entity by receiving technical assistance from Federal entities or other partners, hiring additional employees, or obtaining support from contracted entities; or

children.label: bb
children.text: acquiring additional programmatic information or data, such as through surveys or asset inventories;

children.label: IV
children.text: an explanation of how the needs described in subclause (III) were identified and how funds may be used to address those needs, including target areas;

children.label: V
children.text: details of any relevant partners, such as organizations that may inform broadband deployment and adoption planning; and

children.label: VI
children.text: any other information determined relevant by the Assistant Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall establish a process through which an eligible entity, in submitting a letter of intent under subparagraph (B), may request access to not more than 5 percent of the amount allocated to the eligible entity under subsection (c)(2) for use consistent with this subparagraph.

children.heading: Funding availability
children.label: ii Funding availability
children.text: If the Assistant Secretary approves a request from an eligible entity under clause (i), the Assistant Secretary shall make available to the eligible entity an amount, as determined appropriate by the Assistant Secretary, that is not more than 5 percent of the amount allocated to the eligible entity under subsection (c)(2).

children.label: I
children.text: research and data collection, including initial identification of unserved locations and underserved locations;

children.label: II
children.text: the development of a preliminary budget for pre-planning activities;

children.label: III
children.text: publications, outreach, and communications support;

children.label: IV
children.text: providing technical assistance, including through workshops and events;

children.label: V
children.text: training for employees of the broadband program or office of the eligible entity or employees of political subdivisions of the eligible entity, and related staffing capacity or consulting or contracted support; and

children.label: VI
children.text: with respect to an office that oversees broadband programs and broadband deployment in an eligible entity, establishing, operating, or increasing the capacity of such a broadband office.

children.label: I
children.text: be informed by collaboration with local and regional entities; and

children.label: aa
children.text: investment priorities and associated costs;

children.label: bb
children.text: alignment of planned spending with economic development, telehealth, and related connectivity efforts.

children.label: I
children.text: address local and regional needs in the eligible entity with respect to broadband service;

children.label: II
children.text: propose solutions for the deployment of affordable broadband service in the eligible entity;

children.label: III
children.text: include localized data with respect to the deployment of broadband service in the eligible entity, including by identifying locations that should be prioritized for Federal support with respect to that deployment;

children.label: IV
children.text: ascertain how best to serve unserved locations in the eligible entity, whether through the establishment of cooperatives or public-private partnerships;

children.label: V
children.text: identify the technical assistance that would be necessary to carry out the plan; and

children.label: VI
children.text: assess the amount of time it would take to build out universal broadband service in the eligible entity.

children.heading: Notice of available amounts; invitation to submit initial and final proposals
children.label: 2 Notice of available amounts; invitation to submit initial and final proposals
A
children.text: contains the estimated amount available to the eligible entity under subsection (c); and

children.label: B
children.text: invites the eligible entity to submit an initial proposal and final proposal for a grant under this section, in accordance with paragraphs (3) and (4) of this subsection.

children.label: aa
children.text: information developed by the eligible entity as part of the action plan submitted under paragraph (1)(D), if applicable; and

children.label: bb
children.text: information from any comparable strategic plan otherwise developed by the eligible entity, if applicable;

children.label: aa
children.text: identifies, and outlines steps to support, local and regional broadband planning processes or ongoing efforts to deploy broadband or close the digital divide; and

children.label: bb
children.text: describes coordination with local governments, along with local and regional broadband planning processes;

children.label: III
children.text: identifies existing efforts funded by the Federal Government or a State within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity to deploy broadband and close the digital divide;

children.label: IV
children.text: includes a plan to competitively award subgrants to ensure timely deployment of broadband;

children.label: aa
children.text: each unserved location or underserved location under the jurisdiction of the eligible entity; and

children.label: bb
children.text: each community anchor institution under the jurisdiction of the eligible entity that is an eligible community anchor institution; and

children.label: VI
children.text: certifies the intent of the eligible entity to comply with all applicable requirements under this section, including the reporting requirements under subsection (j)(1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall establish local coordination requirements for eligible entities to follow, to the greatest extent practicable.

children.label: aa
children.text: submit plans for consideration by the eligible entity; and

children.label: bb
children.text: comment on the initial proposal of the eligible entity before the initial proposal is submitted to the Assistant Secretary.

children.heading: Single initial proposal
children.label: B Single initial proposal
children.text: An eligible entity may submit only 1 initial proposal under this paragraph.

children.heading: Corrections to initial proposal
children.label: C Corrections to initial proposal
children.text: The Assistant Secretary may accept corrections to the initial proposal of an eligible entity after the initial proposal has been submitted.

children.label: i
children.text: shall acknowledge receipt;

children.label: aa
children.text: complies with subsection (f);

children.label: bb
children.text: is in the public interest; and

children.label: cc
children.text: effectuates the purposes of this Act;

children.label: II
children.text: shall approve or disapprove the initial proposal based on the determinations under subclause (I); and

children.label: aa
children.text: 20 percent of the grant funds that were allocated to the eligible entity under subsection (c); or

children.label: bb
children.text: a higher percentage of the grant funds that were allocated to the eligible entity under subsection (c), at the discretion of the Assistant Secretary; and

children.label: iii
children.text: if the initial proposal is incomplete, or is disapproved under clause (ii)(II), shall notify the eligible entity and provide the eligible entity with an opportunity to resubmit the initial proposal.

children.heading: Consideration of resubmitted initial proposal
children.label: E Consideration of resubmitted initial proposal
i
children.text: shall acknowledge receipt;

children.label: aa
children.text: complies with subsection (f);

children.label: bb
children.text: is in the public interest; and

children.label: cc
children.text: effectuates the purposes of this Act;

children.label: II
children.text: shall approve or disapprove the initial proposal based on the determinations under subclause (I); and

children.label: aa
children.text: 20 percent of the grant funds that were allocated to the eligible entity under subsection (c); or

children.label: bb
children.text: a higher percentage of the grant funds that were allocated to the eligible entity under subsection (c), at the discretion of the Assistant Secretary; and

children.label: iii
children.text: if the initial proposal is incomplete, or is disapproved under clause (ii)(II), shall notify the eligible entity and provide the eligible entity with an opportunity to resubmit the initial proposal.

children.label: aa
children.text: allocate grant funds for the deployment of broadband networks to unserved locations and underserved locations, in accordance with subsection (h)(1)(A)(i); and

children.label: bb
children.text: align the grant funds allocated to the eligible entity under subsection (c), where practicable, with the use of other funds that the eligible entity receives from the Federal Government, a State, or a private entity for related purposes;

children.label: II
children.text: a timeline for implementation;

children.label: III
children.text: processes for oversight and accountability to ensure the proper use of the grant funds allocated to the eligible entity under subsection (c); and

children.label: IV
children.text: a description of coordination with local governments, along with local and regional broadband planning processes.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall establish local coordination requirements for eligible entities to follow, to the greatest extent practicable.

children.label: aa
children.text: submit plans for consideration by the eligible entity; and

children.label: bb
children.text: comment on the final proposal of the eligible entity before the final proposal is submitted to the Assistant Secretary.

children.heading: Federal coordination
children.label: iii Federal coordination
children.text: To ensure efficient and effective use of taxpayer funds, an eligible entity shall, to the greatest extent practicable, align the use of grant funds proposed in the final proposal under clause (i) with funds available from other Federal programs that support broadband deployment and access.

children.heading: Single final proposal
children.label: B Single final proposal
children.text: An eligible entity may submit only 1 final proposal under this paragraph.

children.heading: Corrections to final proposal
children.label: C Corrections to final proposal
children.text: The Assistant Secretary may accept corrections to the final proposal of an eligible entity after the final proposal has been submitted.

children.label: i
children.text: shall acknowledge receipt;

children.label: aa
children.text: complies with subsection (f);

children.label: bb
children.text: is in the public interest; and

children.label: cc
children.text: effectuates the purposes of this Act;

children.label: II
children.text: shall approve or disapprove the final proposal based on the determinations under subclause (I); and

children.label: III
children.text: if the Assistant Secretary approves the final proposal under clause (ii)(II), shall make available to the eligible entity the remainder of the grant funds allocated to the eligible entity under subsection (c); and

children.label: iii
children.text: if the final proposal is incomplete, or is disapproved under clause (ii)(II), shall notify the eligible entity and provide the eligible entity with an opportunity to resubmit the final proposal.

children.heading: Consideration of resubmitted final proposal
children.label: E Consideration of resubmitted final proposal
i
children.text: shall acknowledge receipt;

children.label: aa
children.text: complies with subsection (f);

children.label: bb
children.text: is in the public interest; and

children.label: cc
children.text: effectuates the purposes of this Act;

children.label: II
children.text: shall approve or disapprove the final proposal based on the determinations under subclause (I); and

children.label: III

children.label: iii
children.text: if the final proposal is incomplete, or is disapproved under clause (ii)(II), shall notify the eligible entity and provide the eligible entity with an opportunity to resubmit the final proposal.
unserved service projects and underserved service projects;
connecting eligible community anchor institutions;
data collection, broadband mapping, and planning;

children.label: A
children.text: has a substantial share of unserved households; or

children.label: B
children.text: is in a location in which the percentage of individuals with a household income that is at or below 150 percent of the poverty line applicable to a family of the size involved (as determined under ) is higher than the national percentage of such individuals;section 9902(2) of title 42
broadband adoption, including programs to provide affordable internet-capable devices; and
any use determined necessary by the Assistant Secretary to facilitate the goals of the Program.

children.label: A
children.text: shall adhere to quality-of-service standards, as established by the Assistant Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: shall comply with prudent cybersecurity and supply chain risk management practices, as specified by the Assistant Secretary, in consultation with the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology and the Commission;

children.label: C
children.text: shall incorporate best practices, as defined by the Assistant Secretary, for ensuring reliability and resilience of broadband infrastructure; and

children.label: i
children.text: any covered communications equipment or service, as defined in ; orsection 1608 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: fiber optic cable and optical transmission equipment manufactured in the People’s Republic of China, except that the Assistant Secretary may waive the application of this clause with respect to a project if the eligible entity that awards a subgrant for the project shows that such application would unreasonably increase the cost of the project.

children.label: i
children.text: is capable of carrying out activities funded by the subgrant in a competent manner in compliance with all applicable Federal, State, and local laws;

children.label: I
children.text: the commitments of the subgrantee under the subgrant;

children.label: II
children.text: the requirements of the Program; and

children.label: III
children.text: such requirements as may be further prescribed by the Assistant Secretary; and

children.label: iii
children.text: has the technical and operational capability to provide the services promised in the subgrant in the manner contemplated by the subgrant award;

children.label: B
children.text: stipulate, in any contract with a subgrantee for the use of such funds, reasonable provisions for recovery of funds for nonperformance; and

children.label: i
children.text: distribute the funds in an equitable and non-discriminatory manner; and

children.label: ii
children.text: ensure, through a stipulation in any contract with a subgrantee for the use of such funds, that each subgrantee uses the funds in an equitable and nondiscriminatory manner.

children.label: A
children.text: shall establish, in coordination with relevant Federal and State partners, appropriate mechanisms to ensure appropriate use of funds made available under this section;

children.label: I
children.text: violates paragraph (2); or

children.label: II
children.text: demonstrates an insufficient level of performance, or wasteful or fraudulent spending, as defined in advance by the Assistant Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: award grant funds that are deobligated under clause (i) to new or existing applicants consistent with this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: shall create and maintain a fully searchable database, accessible on the internet at no cost to the public, that contains information sufficient to allow the public to understand and monitor grants and subgrants awarded under the Program.

children.label: I
children.text: prioritizes unserved service projects;

children.label: II
children.text: after certifying to the Assistant Secretary that the eligible entity will ensure coverage of broadband service to all unserved locations within the eligible entity, prioritizes underserved service projects; and

children.label: III
children.text: after prioritizing underserved service projects, provides funding to connect eligible community anchor institutions;

children.label: ii
children.text: in providing funding under subclauses (I), (II), and (III) of clause (i), shall prioritize funding for deployment of broadband infrastructure for priority broadband projects;

children.label: iii
children.text: may not exclude cooperatives, nonprofit organizations, public-private partnerships, private companies, public or private utilities, public utility districts, or local governments from eligibility for such grant funds; and

children.label: I
children.text: deployment of a broadband network to persistent poverty counties or high-poverty areas;

children.label: II
children.text: the speeds of the proposed broadband service;

children.label: III
children.text: the expediency with which a project can be completed; and

children.label: IV
children.text: a demonstrated record of and plans to be in compliance with Federal labor and employment laws.

children.heading: Authority of Assistant Secretary
children.label: B Authority of Assistant Secretary
children.text: The Assistant Secretary may provide additional guidance on the prioritization of subgrants awarded for the deployment of a broadband network using grant funds received under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: After submitting an initial proposal under subsection (e)(3) and before allocating grant funds received under this section for the deployment of broadband networks, an eligible entity shall ensure a transparent, evidence-based, and expeditious challenge process under which a unit of local government, nonprofit organization, or other broadband service provider can challenge a determination made by the eligible entity in the initial proposal as to whether a particular location or community anchor institution within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity is eligible for the grant funds, including whether a particular location is unserved or underserved.

children.heading: Final identification; notification of funding eligibility
children.label: B Final identification; notification of funding eligibility
children.text: After resolving each challenge under subparagraph (A), and not later than 60 days before allocating grant funds received under this section for the deployment of broadband networks, an eligible entity shall provide public notice of the final classification of each unserved location, underserved location, or eligible community anchor institution within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity.

children.heading: Consultation with NTIA
children.label: C Consultation with NTIA
children.text: An eligible entity shall notify the Assistant Secretary of any modification to the initial proposal of the eligible entity submitted under subsection (e)(3) that is necessitated by a successful challenge under subparagraph (A) of this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: may modify the challenge process required under subparagraph (A) as necessary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may reverse the determination of an eligible entity with respect to the eligibility of a particular location or community anchor institution for grant funds under this section.

children.heading: Expediting broadband data collection activities
children.label: E Expediting broadband data collection activities

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: iii Implementation
children.text: The Commission shall implement the amendments made by this subparagraph as soon as possible after .November 15, 2021

children.heading: Non-Federal share of broadband infrastructure deployment costs
children.label: 3 Non-Federal share of broadband infrastructure deployment costs

children.heading: Matching requirement
children.label: i Matching requirement
children.text: In allocating grant funds received under this section for deployment of broadband networks, an eligible entity shall provide, or require a subgrantee to provide, a contribution, derived from non-Federal funds (or funds from a Federal regional commission or authority), except in high-cost areas or as otherwise provided by this Act, of not less than 25 percent of project costs.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: ii Waiver
children.text: Upon request by an eligible entity or a subgrantee, the Assistant Secretary may reduce or waive the required matching contribution under clause (i).

children.label: i
children.text: may be provided by an eligible entity, a unit of local government, a utility company, a cooperative, a nonprofit organization, a for-profit company, regional planning or governmental organization, a Federal regional commission or authority, or any combination thereof;

children.label: ii
children.text: may include in-kind contributions; and

children.label: aa
children.text: the Families First Coronavirus Response Act (; );Public Law 116–127134 Stat. 178

children.label: bb
children.text: the CARES Act (; );Public Law 116–136134 Stat. 281

children.label: cc
children.text: the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2021 (; );Public Law 116–260134 Stat. 1182

children.label: dd
children.text: the American Rescue Plan Act of 2021 (; ); orPublic Law 117–2135 Stat. 4

children.label: ee
children.text: any amendment made by an Act described in any of items (aa) through (dd); and

children.label: II
children.text: for the purpose of deployment of broadband service, as described in the applicable provision of law described in subclause (I).

children.label: i
children.text: the Appalachian Regional Commission;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Delta Regional Authority;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Denali Commission; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the Northern Border Regional Commission.

children.heading: Deployment and provision of service requirements
children.label: 4 Deployment and provision of service requirements
I
children.text: at a speed of not less than 100 megabits per second for downloads and 20 megabits per second for uploads;

children.label: II
children.text: with a latency that is sufficiently low to allow reasonably foreseeable, real-time, interactive applications; and

children.label: III
children.text: with network outages that do not exceed, on average, 48 hours over any 365-day period; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall provide access to broadband service to each customer served by the project that desires broadband service;

children.label: B
children.text: shall offer not less than 1 low-cost broadband service option for eligible subscribers, as those terms are defined in paragraph (5) of this subsection;

children.label: i
children.text: the eligible entity has a plan for use of the grant funds;

children.label: ii
children.text: the construction project is underway; or

children.label: iii
children.text: extenuating circumstances require an extension of time to allow the project to be completed;

children.label: D
children.text: for any project that involves laying fiber optic cables or conduit underground or along a roadway, shall include interspersed conduit access points at regular and short intervals;

children.label: E
children.text: may use the subgrant to deploy broadband infrastructure in or through any area required to reach interconnection points or otherwise to ensure the technical feasibility and financial sustainability of a project providing broadband service to an unserved location, underserved location, or eligible community anchor institution;

children.label: F
children.text: once the network has been deployed, shall provide public notice, online and through other means, of that fact to the locations and areas to which broadband service has been provided and share the public notice with the eligible entity that awarded the subgrant;

children.label: G
children.text: shall carry out public awareness campaigns in service areas that are designed to highlight the value and benefits of broadband service in order to increase the adoption of broadband service by consumers; and

children.label: H
children.text: if the entity is no longer able to provide broadband service to the locations covered by the subgrant at any time, shall sell the network capacity at a reasonable, wholesale rate on a nondiscriminatory basis to other broadband service providers or public sector entities.

children.label: i
children.text: the term “eligible subscriber” shall have the meaning given the term by the Assistant Secretary for purposes of this paragraph; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the term “low-cost broadband service option” shall be defined by an eligible entity for subgrantees of the eligible entity in accordance with subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Proposal
children.label: i Proposal
children.text: An eligible entity shall submit to the Assistant Secretary for approval, in the final proposal of the eligible entity submitted under subsection (e)(4), a proposed definition of “low-cost broadband service option” that shall apply to subgrantees of the eligible entity for purposes of the requirement under paragraph (4)(B) of this subsection.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: ii Consultation
children.text: An eligible entity shall consult with the Assistant Secretary and prospective subgrantees regarding a proposed definition of “low-cost broadband service option” before submitting the proposed definition to the Assistant Secretary under clause (i).

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: A proposed definition of “low-cost broadband service option” submitted by an eligible entity under clause (i) shall not take effect until the Assistant Secretary approves the final proposal of the eligible entity submitted under subsection (e)(4), including approval of the proposed definition of “low-cost broadband service option”.

children.label: aa
children.text: notify the eligible entity and provide the eligible entity with an opportunity to resubmit the final proposal, as provided in subsection (e)(4), with an improved definition of “low-cost broadband service option”; and

children.label: bb
children.text: provide the eligible entity with instructions on how to cure the defects in the proposed definition.

children.heading: Public disclosure
children.label: iv Public disclosure
children.text: After the Assistant Secretary approves the final proposal of an eligible entity under subsection (e)(4), and before the Assistant Secretary disburses any funds to the eligible entity based on that approval, the Assistant Secretary shall publicly disclose the eligible entity’s definition of “low-cost broadband service option”.

children.label: i
children.text: evaluate the compliance of a subgrantee with the requirement under paragraph (4)(B); and

children.label: ii
children.text: take corrective action, including recoupment of funds from the subgrantee, for noncompliance with the requirement under paragraph (4)(B).

children.heading: No regulation of rates permitted
children.label: D No regulation of rates permitted
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter may be construed to authorize the Assistant Secretary or the National Telecommunications and Information Administration to regulate the rates charged for broadband service.

children.heading: Guidance
children.label: E Guidance
children.text: The Assistant Secretary may issue guidance to eligible entities to carry out the purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Return of funds
children.label: 6 Return of funds
children.text: An entity that receives a subgrant from an eligible entity under subsection (f) and fails to comply with any requirement under this subsection shall return up to the entire amount of the subgrant to the eligible entity, at the discretion of the eligible entity or the Assistant Secretary.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: i Regulations
children.text: The Assistant Secretary may issue such regulations or other guidance, forms, instructions, and publications as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the programs, projects, or activities authorized under this section, including to ensure that those programs, projects, or activities are completed in a timely and effective manner.

children.label: i
children.text: describes the planned and actual use of funds;

children.label: ii
children.text: describes the planned and actual process of subgranting;

children.label: iii
children.text: identifies the establishment of appropriate mechanisms by the eligible entity to ensure that all subgrantees of the eligible entity comply with the eligible uses prescribed under subsection (f); and

children.label: iv
children.text: includes any other information required by the Assistant Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: describes how the eligible entity expended the grant funds;

children.label: ii
children.text: describes each service provided with the grant funds;

children.label: iii
children.text: describes the number of locations at which broadband service was made available using the grant funds, and the number of those locations at which broadband service was utilized; and

children.label: iv
children.text: certifies that the eligible entity complied with the requirements of this section and with any additional reporting requirements prescribed by the Assistant Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: describes how the eligible entity expended the funds;

children.label: ii
children.text: describes each service provided with the grant funds;

children.label: iii
children.text: describes the number of locations at which broadband service was made available using the grant funds, and the number of those locations at which broadband service was utilized;

children.label: iv
children.text: includes each report that the eligible entity received from a subgrantee under paragraph (2); and

children.label: v
children.text: certifies that the eligible entity complied with the requirements of this section and with any additional reporting requirements prescribed by the Assistant Secretary.

children.heading: Provision to FCC and USDA
children.label: D Provision to FCC and USDA
children.text: Subject to section 904(b)(2) of division FF of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2021 () [] (relating to an interagency agreement), the Assistant Secretary shall coordinate with the Commission and the Department of Agriculture, including providing the final reports received under subparagraph (C) to the Commission and the Department of Agriculture to be used when determining whether to award funds for the deployment of broadband under any program administered by those agencies.Public Law 116–26047 U.S.C. 1308(b)(2)

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: i Definitions
children.text: In this subparagraph, the terms “agency” and “Federal broadband support program” have the meanings given those terms in section 903 of division FF of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2021 () [] (also known as the “ACCESS BROADBAND Act”).Public Law 116–26047 U.S.C. 1307

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: ii Requirement
children.text: An agency that offers a Federal broadband support program shall provide data to the Assistant Secretary, in a manner and format prescribed by the Assistant Secretary, to promote coordination of efforts to track construction and use of broadband infrastructure.

children.heading: Semiannual report
children.label: A Semiannual report
children.text: The recipient of a subgrant from an eligible entity under this section shall submit to the eligible entity a semiannual report for the duration of the subgrant to track the effectiveness of the use of funds provided.

children.label: i
children.text: describe each type of project carried out using the subgrant and the duration of the subgrant;

children.label: I
children.text: include a list of addresses or locations that constitute the service locations that will be served by the broadband infrastructure to be constructed;

children.label: II
children.text: identify whether each address or location described in subclause (I) is residential, commercial, or a community anchor institution;

children.label: III
children.text: describe the types of facilities that have been constructed and installed;

children.label: IV
children.text: describe the peak and off-peak actual speeds of the broadband service being offered;

children.label: V
children.text: describe the maximum advertised speed of the broadband service being offered;

children.label: VI
children.text: describe the non-promotional prices, including any associated fees, charged for different tiers of broadband service being offered;

children.label: VII
children.text: include any other data that would be required to comply with the data and mapping collection standards of the Commission under section 1.7004 of title 47, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulation, for broadband infrastructure projects; and

children.label: VIII
children.text: comply with any other reasonable reporting requirements determined by the eligible entity or the Assistant Secretary; and

children.label: iii
children.text: certify that the information in the report is accurate.

children.label: A
children.text: standardize and coordinate reporting of locations at which broadband service was provided using grant funds received under this section in accordance with title VIII of the Communications Act of 1934 ( et seq.); and47 U.S.C. 641

children.label: B
children.text: provide a standardized methodology to recipients of grants and subgrantees under this section for reporting the information described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Information on broadband subsidies and low-income plans
children.label: 4 Information on broadband subsidies and low-income plans
I
children.text: to receive a Federal or State subsidy with respect to broadband service; or

children.label: II
children.text: for a low-income plan with respect to broadband service; and

children.label: ii
children.text: contains information regarding how to apply for the applicable benefit described in clause (i).

children.heading: Provision of data
children.label: B Provision of data
children.text: A Federal entity, State entity receiving Federal funds, or provider of broadband service that offers a subsidy or low-income plan, as applicable, with respect to broadband service shall provide data to the Assistant Secretary in a manner and format as established by the Assistant Secretary as necessary for the Assistant Secretary to carry out subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Relation to other public funding
children.label: k Relation to other public funding

children.text: an entity that has received amounts from the Federal Government or a State or local government for the purpose of expanding access to broadband service may receive a subgrant under subsection (f) in accordance with this section; and

the receipt of a subgrant under subsection (f) by an entity described in paragraph (1) of this subsection shall not affect the eligibility of the entity to receive the amounts from the Federal Government or a State or local government described in that paragraph.

children.heading: Supplement not supplant
children.label: l Supplement not supplant
children.text: Grant funds awarded to an eligible entity under this section shall be used to supplement, and not supplant, the amounts that the eligible entity would otherwise make available for the purposes for which the grant funds may be used.

children.heading: Sense of Congress regarding Federal agency coordination
children.label: m Sense of Congress regarding Federal agency coordination
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that Federal agencies responsible for supporting broadband deployment, including the Commission, the Department of Commerce, and the Department of Agriculture, to the extent possible, should align the goals, application and reporting processes, and project requirements with respect to broadband deployment supported by those agencies.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The United States District Court for the District of Columbia shall have exclusive jurisdiction to review a decision of the Assistant Secretary made under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the decision was procured by corruption, fraud, or undue means;

children.label: B
children.text: there was actual partiality or corruption in the Assistant Secretary; or

children.label: i
children.text: misconduct in refusing to review the administrative record; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any other misbehavior by which the rights of any party have been prejudiced.
(commonly referred to as the “Paperwork Reduction Act”);section 3506 of title 44
chapter 5 or 7 of title 5 (commonly referred to as the “Administrative Procedures Act”); and
chapter 6 of title 5 (commonly referred to as the “Regulatory Flexibility Act”).
